But what I see across Europe today is that allies are now providing a massive amount of support—logistics, basing, and other measures.

Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan: The ceasefire was declared yesterday, but even before moving on to negotiations, the first problem immediately emerged. As expected, Israel acted as a spoiler and carried out a large-scale air operation against Lebanon — something that had not happened up until that point — causing the deaths of many civilians. Now, under the shadow of this provocation by Israel, the negotiations that are supposed to continue will naturally be very difficult. I would like to remind you that the issues to be discussed are extremely complex. Many of these topics have already been debated frequently in previous talks between the United States and Iran. However, even now, we can see that there are still fluctuations and uncertainties, particularly regarding enrichment. The nuclear issue remains the most heavily debated topic, and even there, differences of opinion may persist.

🇮🇷 | ResistanceTrench Statement on the 40th Day of the Martyrdom of Ayatollah Imam Khamenei On this solemn day, marking forty days since the martyrdom of Ayatollah Imam Khamenei (may Allah’s mercy be upon him), the Ummah reflects upon a loss that shook the foundations of the Islamic world—yet strengthened its روح of resistance and faith. Indeed, Allah ﷻ is the Ultimate Power, the One who grants victory and عزّة to His sincere servants. The martyrdom of such a leader is not an end, but a continuation of a divine path written by sacrifice, patience, and unwavering ایمان. The Islamic Republic of Iran, through its institutions and especially the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), has reaffirmed that the blood of the martyrs does not weaken the الأمة—it revives it. The legacy of Imam Khamenei lives on through resistance, unity, and steadfastness against oppression and arrogance. His life and martyrdom have become a turning point—fueling determination across the resistance front, from Iran to Palestine and beyond. The enemies sought to silence a voice, but instead ignited a movement that continues to grow stronger with each passing day. The principles he upheld—justice, independence, unity, and defiance against tyranny—remain a guiding light for all those who walk the path of resistance. His legacy is not confined to history; it lives in every act of defiance, every stand for truth, and every believer who refuses submission to falsehood. As emphasized in official statements, including those of the IRGC, the path of the martyrs will not be abandoned. Rather, it will continue with renewed strength under steadfast leadership, and with the وعد of Allah that truth will prevail over falsehood. On this 40th day, ResistanceTrench honors the memory of the martyred leader and all those who stood beside him. The عهد remains unbroken, the path remains clear, and the struggle continues until justice is established. 🫡

Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan: The international community must now clearly recognize this reality: as long as Israel’s expansionism continues, it will not be possible to build lasting peace, stability, and security in the Middle East. Israel must be stopped from imposing its geopolitical ambitions on the region through external interventions. The Netanyahu government continues to condemn the people of Gaza to famine under the pretext of war, systematically restricts freedom of worship in East Jerusalem, and adds new unlawful measures each day aimed at undermining the two-state solution in the West Bank. Israel is now extending what it has done in Gaza to Lebanon. Before the ceasefire framework can even be properly established, ongoing Israeli attacks in Lebanon — carried out without regard for children or civilians — are causing hundreds of casualties and pushing the region into a deeper humanitarian crisis. Ending Israel’s occupation in Lebanon and protecting civilians has become an urgent and non-negotiable priority.

Survivors of the deadly Iranian drone strike in Kuwait are challenging the Pentagon’s version of events, saying their unit “was unprepared” and not properly protected. One injured soldier rejected the official account, stating: “Painting a picture that ‘one squeaked through’ is a falsehood… the unit was unprepared to provide any defense for itself. It was not a fortified position.” Troops described being stationed at a lightly protected site despite knowing it was a potential target: “We moved closer to Iran, to a deeply unsafe area that was a known target… I don’t think there was a good reason ever articulated.” Some believe the attack could have been avoided. “Telling the truth is important… we’re not going to learn from these mistakes if we pretend these mistakes didn’t happen,” one said, adding the strike was “absolutely” preventable. Source: CBS News

🇩🇪 🇺🇸 German chancellor Friedrich Merz said he did not want the US-Israeli campaign in Iran to “split” Nato or put further pressure on the relations between the US and its European allies, as he suggested Germany could play a role in securing the strait of Hormuz. “Nato is a guarantor of our security, including and above all in Europe,” he stressed. Merz separately confirmed plans to help with securing the strait of Hormuz, but stressed that any such mission would “ideally” have the backing of the UN security council and of the German parliament, so “no decisions are to be expected within a few days.” 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

German Chancellor Merz: We did not discuss any withdrawal of U.S. soldiers, nor any restriction on the use of military infrastructure maintained by the American armed forces in Germany. There are stationing agreements in place, and the federal government has no reason to doubt that the American forces have acted within the framework of these agreements in recent weeks.
